FY.executive_summary: Apple Inc.'s (AAPL) latest fiscal year report showcases a strong top-line performance, with revenue growing 6.4% to $416.2B USD. This growth translates to a significant increase in net income, which surged 19.5% to $112.0B USD. The company's profitability has seen a notable uptick, with net margin expanding by 12.3% to 26.9%, while return on equity (ROE) decreased by 7.7% to 151.9%. Despite the decline in ROE, Apple's profitability remains robust. On the other hand, the company's leverage has decreased, with debt-to-equity decreasing by 28.4% to 3.87x. Apple's liquidity position also improved, with the current ratio increasing by 3.0% to 0.89. In the past month, the stock price has risen 10.3% to $327.74 USD, indicating investor confidence in the company's financial health. Overall, Apple's financial performance suggests a resilient business model, with a strong revenue engine and manageable leverage.
